name: Sonoran Fry Bread
recipeIngredient: freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the flour, baking powder, salt, pepper, and cumin.
2. Slowly add water until a soft dough forms; it should not be sticky.
3. Knead gently and let rest for 15 minutes.
4. Heat the oil to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Roll the dough into a sheet that is 1/2 inch thick.
6. Cut into 3-inch square or round pieces.
7. Drop several pieces at a time into hot oil and cook until they float to the top.
8. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain.
"Prepared at the San Luis Resort and Conference Center in Galveston during this year's Mardi Gras tribute to Mexico."
By Lou Parris  on May 13, 1997
